disclaimer: Alterations and therapeutics are aggregated from public databases (Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, DGIdb, and others). Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: NMIBC: 50-70% recurrence within 5 years; MIBC: progression to metastatic in 50% without radical treatment; spontaneous remission essentially zero
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: NMIBC: BCG intravesical immunotherapy: 70% reduction in recurrence, 30-40% reduction in progression; MIBC with neoadjuvant cisplatin + radical cystectomy: 5-year survival 50-60% (vs 45% cystectomy alone); metastatic with enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab: 31.5 months OS vs 16.1 months cisplatin (EV-302/KEYNOTE-A39)
remission.gap_size: Large — EV + pembrolizumab is a major advance but not yet universally standard; BCG supply shortage is a persistent clinical crisis in NMIBC
remission.barrier_detail: BCG shortage (since 2012, persistent) means many NMIBC patients cannot receive standard treatment; gemcitabine + mitomycin as alternative to BCG established as FDA-cleared 2024 (CISTO trial); EV + pembrolizumab is a genuine paradigm change for metastatic disease but requires PD-L1 testing integration
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — EV + pembrolizumab (EV-302) established as first-line for cisplatin-eligible metastatic UC; 2022 pembrolizumab second-line established; 2019 enfortumab vedotin approved; BCG standard for NMIBC since 1990
remission.outdated_treatments: MVAC chemotherapy in cisplatin-eligible patients (gemcitabine + cisplatin equivalent efficacy with better tolerability — largely replaced but MVAC persists in some centres); maintenance BCG without checking BCG availability and dosing schedule; radical cystectomy without neoadjuvant chemotherapy discussion in MIBC
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Bladder preservation (trimodal therapy — maximal TURBT + chemoradiation): 5-year survival equivalent to cystectomy in selected patients; exercise and stomal care after cystectomy; FGFR inhibitors (erdafitinib — FGFR-altered UC)
remission.frontier_signal: Enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab becoming first-line standard; sacituzumab govitecan for UC (TROPHY-U-01 Phase 2 positive); erdafitinib (FGFR2/3 targeted — Phase 3 THOR positive vs chemo in FGFR-altered); CLDN18.2 targeting; TAR-200 (intravesical gemcitabine releasing device for BCG-unresponsive NMIBC)
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
